task_completion.description: Create at least three interior partition walls and three doors to enclose the zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan pane (top right) or 3D view (bottom right), verify there are at least 3 new interior walls dividing the open shell and at least 3 doors placed within the walls.

task_completion.description: Create and name four specific rooms: Vestibule/Gowning Room, Main Grow Room, Nutrient/Utility Room, and Harvesting & Packaging Area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan pane, look for room area overlays with text labels matching or closely approximating the four required names.

task_completion.description: Apply distinct floor colors or textures to at least two rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D view pane or 2D plan, verify that at least two of the defined room areas have different floor colors or textures from each other.

task_completion.description: Place at least 15 shelving units in the Grow Room and 2 cabinets in the Vestibule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the furniture list pane (bottom left) and the 2D/3D views. Count at least 15 shelving units located in the Main Grow Room area and at least 2 cabinets in the Vestibule area.

task_completion.description: Place at least 3 workbenches in the Packaging Area and 2 utility fixtures in the Nutrient Room.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the furniture list pane and the 2D/3D views. Count at least 3 workbenches/tables in the Harvesting & Packaging Area and at least 2 utility fixtures (e.g., boiler, water heater, sink) in the Nutrient/Utility Room.

Check the Sweet Home 3D window title bar at the top of the screen; it should display a saved filename with a .sh3d extension instead of 'Untitled', or a save dialog/confirmation should be visible in the final screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the Sweet Home 3D tools (drawing walls, dragging furniture from the catalog), rather than terminal commands or text editors modifying the save file directly.

integrity.description: The layout was built progressively over time.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots shows walls, rooms, and furniture being added step-by-step, rather than a fully completed layout appearing instantly from a loaded file.

integrity.description: The 3D view accurately reflects the 2D plan additions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bottom 3D view pane updates dynamically to show the 3D models of the walls, doors, and furniture placed in the 2D plan, confirming genuine software rendering.
